content:
The biggest bottleneck in modern software engineering is no longer writing code. It’s deciding what code should exist in the first place.

6 min read
Just now

--

Press enter or click to view image in full size

AI made code generation nearly free. The new engineering advantage is judgment: architecture, tradeoffs, reliability, scalability, and system design.
Code Became Cheap. Judgment Became Expensive.
A few years ago, engineering teams competed on typing speed.
Today, they compete on decision quality.
That’s a strange thing to say at a moment when AI can generate API endpoints, database migrations, Docker files, integration tests, Terraform modules, and entire applications in minutes.
But that’s exactly why judgment suddenly matters more than ever.
When code was expensive, good engineers were valued for producing it.
When code became cheap, good engineers became valuable for rejecting it.
The industry is slowly realizing something uncomfortable:
The hardest part of software was never writing code.
